ui.subtitle: Quick reference for cards & rules
ui.footer: Community reference — not affiliated with Catan Studio.

base.name: Catan (Base Game)
base.description: The classic Settlers of Catan. Collect resources, build roads, settlements and cities, trade with other players and be the first to reach 10 Victory Points.

cards.name: Lumber
cards.description: Produced by Forest hexes when their number is rolled.
usages: Build a Road (Lumber + Brick)
Build a Settlement (Lumber + Brick + Wool + Grain)

cards.name: Brick
cards.description: Produced by Hills hexes when their number is rolled.
usages: Build a Road (Lumber + Brick)
Build a Settlement (Lumber + Brick + Wool + Grain)

cards.name: Wool
cards.description: Produced by Pasture hexes when their number is rolled.
usages: Build a Settlement (Lumber + Brick + Wool + Grain)
Build a City (× in City cost)
Buy a Development Card (Ore + Grain + Wool)

cards.name: Grain
cards.description: Produced by Fields hexes when their number is rolled.
usages: Build a Settlement (Lumber + Brick + Wool + Grain)
Upgrade to City (Ore×3 + Grain×2)
Buy a Development Card (Ore + Grain + Wool)

cards.name: Ore
cards.description: Produced by Mountain hexes when their number is rolled.
usages: Upgrade to City (Ore×3 + Grain×2)
Buy a Development Card (Ore + Grain + Wool)

cards.name: Knight
cards.description: Move the Robber to any terrain hex. Steal one resource card from an adjacent opponent.
usages: Activate before rolling the dice
Collect Largest Army card when you play 3 knights (2 VP)

cards.name: Road Building
cards.description: Place 2 new roads on the board as if you had just built them.
usages: Expand your network quickly
Compete for Longest Road (2 VP when ≥5 roads)

cards.name: Year of Plenty
cards.description: Take any 2 resource cards from the bank. Add them to your hand.
usages: Grab the exact resources you need to build
Complete a combination you're one resource short on

cards.name: Monopoly
cards.description: Name any resource type. All other players must give you all of their cards of that type.
usages: Disrupt an opponent on the verge of a big build
Collect a resource you urgently need

cards.name: Chapel
cards.description: Worth 1 Victory Point. Kept secret until you reveal it to win (or until the game ends).
usages: Count towards 10 VP to win
Play all at once to surprise opponents when claiming victory

cards.name: Great Hall
cards.description: Worth 1 Victory Point. Kept secret until you reveal it to win (or until the game ends).
usages: Count towards 10 VP to win
Play all at once to surprise opponents when claiming victory

cards.name: Library
cards.description: Worth 1 Victory Point. Kept secret until you reveal it to win (or until the game ends).
usages: Count towards 10 VP to win
Play all at once to surprise opponents when claiming victory

cards.name: Market
cards.description: Worth 1 Victory Point. Kept secret until you reveal it to win (or until the game ends).
usages: Count towards 10 VP to win
Play all at once to surprise opponents when claiming victory

cards.name: University
cards.description: Worth 1 Victory Point. Kept secret until you reveal it to win (or until the game ends).
usages: Count towards 10 VP to win
Play all at once to surprise opponents when claiming victory

cards.name: Longest Road
cards.description: Awarded to the first player with a continuous road of 5+ segments. Worth 2 Victory Points. Another player can steal it by building a longer road.
usages: Build roads aggressively to claim the card
Break an opponent's road by placing a settlement in the middle

cards.name: Largest Army
cards.description: Awarded to the first player who plays 3 Knight cards. Worth 2 Victory Points. Another player can steal it by playing more Knights.
usages: Play Knights to control the Robber and earn 2 VP
Protect wheat/ore hexes from the Robber

cards.name: Building Cost Card
cards.description: Reference card showing all construction costs in Catan.
usages: Road: 1 Lumber + 1 Brick
Settlement: 1 Lumber + 1 Brick + 1 Wool + 1 Grain
City: 3 Ore + 2 Grain
Development Card: 1 Ore + 1 Grain + 1 Wool

setup: Assemble the sea frame, place 19 terrain hexes and number discs inside.
Sort resource cards into 5 stacks; shuffle development cards facedown.
Place the Robber on the desert hex.
Each player places 2 settlements and 2 roads (round 1 clockwise, round 2 reverse).
Collect starting resources from hexes adjacent to your second settlement.

turnOverview.title: 1. Production Phase
turnOverview.detail: Optionally play a development card, then roll both dice. All players collect resources from hexes matching the roll. On a 7: players with >7 cards discard half, then move the Robber and steal.

turnOverview.title: 2. Action Phase
turnOverview.detail: Trade with players or the supply (4:1, 3:1, or 2:1 via ports). Build roads, settlements, cities, or buy development cards. Play 1 development card if you didn't before the roll.

keyRules.title: Roads
keyRules.detail: Cost: 1 Lumber + 1 Brick. Must connect to your existing network. Max 15 per player. Worth 0 VP.

keyRules.title: Settlements
keyRules.detail: Cost: 1 Lumber + 1 Brick + 1 Wool + 1 Grain. Must obey distance rule (2 edges from any building). Max 5 per player. Worth 1 VP.

keyRules.title: Cities
keyRules.detail: Cost: 3 Ore + 2 Grain. Upgrade a settlement (produces 2 resources). Max 4 per player. Worth 2 VP.

keyRules.title: Development Cards
keyRules.detail: Cost: 1 Ore + 1 Grain + 1 Wool. Types: Knight (14), Road Building (2), Year of Plenty (2), Monopoly (2), VP cards (5). Cannot play on the turn bought.

keyRules.title: Longest Road
keyRules.detail: First player with 5+ continuous roads gets this tile (2 VP). Can be stolen if another player builds longer.

keyRules.title: Largest Army
keyRules.detail: First player to play 3 Knights gets this tile (2 VP). Can be stolen by playing more.

keyRules.title: Robber (rolling a 7)
keyRules.detail: Players with >7 cards discard half. Active player moves the Robber to a new hex and steals 1 random card from an adjacent player.

keyRules.title: Trading
keyRules.detail: On your turn: trade freely with other players, or trade with the supply at 4:1 (general), 3:1 (any port), or 2:1 (specific port).

scenarios.name: Fixed Setup
scenarios.description: Pre-determined hex and number layout for beginners.
details: Terrain hexes and numbers placed as shown in rulebook
Recommended for first-time players

scenarios.name: Variable Setup
scenarios.description: Randomized board for experienced players.
details: Shuffle sea frame pieces and terrain hexes
Place number discs alphabetically counterclockwise, skip the desert
Players place settlements in two rounds (clockwise then reverse)

seafarers.name: Catan: Seafarers
seafarers.description: Explore an archipelago of islands. Build ships to sail the seas, discover new islands with gold fields, and settle distant shores for bonus Victory Points.

cards.name: Ship
cards.description: Ships extend your trade routes across water hexes. They work like roads on the sea.
usages: Cost: 1 Lumber + 1 Wool
Can be moved (the end ship of an open route) once per turn
Max 15 ships per player

cards.name: Gold Field
cards.description: Special hex that produces a wild resource. When its number is rolled, adjacent players choose any resource they want from the bank.
usages: Highly valuable — worth settling early
Each adjacent settlement yields 1 free resource of choice

cards.name: New Island Bonus
cards.description: In most Seafarers scenarios, placing your first settlement on a new island earns you 1 extra Victory Point.
usages: Plan ship routes to reach distant islands quickly
Multiple players can earn VP from the same island

cards.name: Pirate
cards.description: The Pirate replaces the Robber in Seafarers. Move it to any sea hex to block ships. Steal from an adjacent player with a ship there.
usages: Activated by rolling a 7 or playing a Knight
Blocks ship movement through that hex

setup: Uses a special Seafarers sea frame with multiple islands.
Place terrain hexes, sea hexes, and gold field hexes per scenario map.
Each player receives 15 ships in addition to standard pieces.
Robber and Pirate are both in play.

turnOverview.title: Production & Action
turnOverview.detail: Same as base game, but ships can be built and moved. On a 7, choose to move the Robber OR the Pirate.

turnOverview.title: Ship Movement
turnOverview.detail: Once per turn, move 1 ship that has an open end (not connected on both sides) to a new legal position.

keyRules.title: Ships
keyRules.detail: Cost: 1 Lumber + 1 Wool. Placed on sea hex edges. Count toward Longest Route. Cannot move on the turn built.

keyRules.title: Gold Fields
keyRules.detail: When the number is rolled, each adjacent player chooses any 1 resource (2 for a city).

keyRules.title: Pirate
keyRules.detail: Move to any sea hex. Steal 1 resource from a player with a ship there. Blocks ship placement/movement.

keyRules.title: Longest Route
keyRules.detail: Roads and ships combine into a route, but only connect through your own buildings (settlements/cities).

keyRules.title: Road Building card
keyRules.detail: Can build 2 roads, 2 ships, or 1 of each.

keyRules.title: Knight card
keyRules.detail: Lets you move the Robber OR the Pirate.

scenarios.name: Heading for New Shores
scenarios.description: Settle small islands across the sea.
details: +2 VP for first settlement on each small island

scenarios.name: The Four Islands
scenarios.description: Four separate islands to colonize.
details: +2 VP for first settlement on each unexplored island

scenarios.name: The Fog Islands
scenarios.description: Facedown hexes revealed when you build adjacent.
details: Gain 1 resource on discovery
Unknown terrain adds exploration challenge

scenarios.name: Through the Desert
scenarios.description: A desert divides the island into regions.
details: +2 VP for first settlement in each unexplored region

scenarios.name: The Forgotten Tribe
scenarios.description: Villages on small islands produce cloth tokens.
details: Establish trade routes to villages
Pair of cloth tokens = 1 VP

scenarios.name: Cloth for Catan
scenarios.description: Cloth tokens from village trade routes.
details: Game can also end when 5+ villages are empty
Steal cloth or resources via pirate

scenarios.name: The Pirate Islands
scenarios.description: Build warships and conquer pirate fortresses.
details: Knight cards convert ships to warships
No Longest Route/Largest Army tiles

scenarios.name: The Wonders of Catan
scenarios.description: Claim and build one of 5 Wonders to win.
details: 4 levels, each costs 5 resources
Complete your Wonder OR reach 10+ VP with highest wonder level

scenarios.name: New World
scenarios.description: Fully randomized island layout.
details: +1 VP per settlement on unexplored island

cities-knights.name: Catan: Cities & Knights
cities-knights.description: A deeper strategic experience. Commodities replace some resources, city improvements unlock powerful abilities, and the Barbarians threaten your cities each turn.

cards.name: Cloth
cards.description: Commodity produced by upgraded Pasture cities (Metropolis). Used to advance in the Trade column of city improvements.
usages: Produced by cities on Pasture hexes (doubled roll)
Invest in Trade city improvements

cards.name: Coin
cards.description: Commodity produced by upgraded Mountain cities. Used to advance in the Science column.
usages: Produced by cities on Mountain hexes
Invest in Science city improvements

cards.name: Paper
cards.description: Commodity produced by upgraded Forest cities. Used to advance in the Politics column.
usages: Produced by cities on Forest hexes
Invest in Politics city improvements

cards.name: Crane (Science)
cards.description: This turn, you may build one city improvement for 1 fewer commodity than normal.
usages: Save a commodity when upgrading city

cards.name: Merchant (Trade)
cards.description: Place the Merchant on any terrain hex. You trade that resource at 2:1 until another player takes the Merchant. Also worth 1 VP.
usages: Control a key resource trading rate
Worth 1 VP while you hold it

cards.name: Spy (Politics)
cards.description: Steal one progress card at random from any opponent's hand.
usages: Disrupt opponents, gain a useful card

cards.name: Engineer (Science)
cards.description: Build one city wall for free.
usages: Protect a city from Barbarians without spending resources

cards.name: Barbarian Attack
cards.description: Each turn a ship is rolled, the Barbarian token advances. When it reaches Catan, a battle is resolved. If barbarians win, the player with fewest knights loses a city.
usages: Build and activate Knights to defend
City Walls protect one city from being downgraded

setup: Uses the C&K sea frame with barbarian track.
No base game development cards — replaced by progress cards (3 decks).
No Largest Army tile.
Each player's second starting piece is a city (not a settlement).
Each player gets a city improvement board with 3 tracks and 3 cubes.
Robber starts off the board until the first barbarian attack.

turnOverview.title: 1. Roll Dice + Event Die
turnOverview.detail: Production die determines resources. Event die advances barbarians or triggers progress card draws.

turnOverview.title: 2. Commodities
turnOverview.detail: Cities on mountains produce Coin (+Ore), on pastures produce Cloth (+Wool), on forests produce Paper (+Wood).

turnOverview.title: 3. Action Phase
turnOverview.detail: Trade, build, improve cities, recruit/activate knights, or play progress cards.

keyRules.title: Commodities
keyRules.detail: Cities produce commodities alongside resources: Coin (mountains), Cloth (pastures), Paper (forests). Used to advance city improvement tracks.

keyRules.title: City Improvements
keyRules.detail: 3 tracks — Trade, Science, Politics. Advance by spending commodities. Level 3 unlocks a permanent ability. Level 4 earns a Metropolis (2 VP).

keyRules.title: Metropolis
keyRules.detail: First to level 4 on a track gains that Metropolis (placed on a city, total 4 VP). First to level 5 gets permanent control. Cannot be pillaged.

keyRules.title: Knights
keyRules.detail: 3 strength levels: Basic, Strong, Mighty. Must be recruited then activated (costs 1 Grain + 1 Ore). Can move, displace weaker enemy knights, or chase the robber. All deactivate after barbarian attack.

keyRules.title: Barbarian Attack
keyRules.detail: Barbarian strength = total cities on board. Defender strength = sum of active knights. If barbarians win: player with fewest active knights loses a city (downgraded to settlement). Metropolises are immune.

keyRules.title: City Walls
keyRules.detail: Placed under cities. Each wall adds +2 to your hand limit when a 7 is rolled. Max 3 walls.

keyRules.title: Progress Cards
keyRules.detail: Drawn based on event die + your track level. Hand limit of 4 progress cards. Many unique effects across 3 decks (Trade, Science, Politics).

keyRules.title: The Merchant
keyRules.detail: Placed via progress card on a hex adjacent to your building. Gives 2:1 trade on that resource and is worth 1 VP.

traders-barbarians.name: Catan: Traders & Barbarians
traders-barbarians.description: A collection of five scenarios and variants that add new dimensions to the classic game: trade routes, caravans, barbarian invasions, and more.

cards.name: Caravan
cards.description: In the Traders & Barbarians scenario, caravans carry goods along roads. Delivering goods to the friendly village earns Victory Points.
usages: Move caravans up to 3 road segments per turn
Earn 1 VP per delivered goods token

cards.name: Gold Coin Token
cards.description: Earned by completing trade routes or delivering goods. Can be used like a wild resource or traded for VP in certain scenarios.
usages: Spend as any resource type
Trade for VP at the friendly village

setup: Modular expansion — 4 standalone variants and 5 standalone scenarios.
Variants and scenarios can be mixed and matched.
Includes a 2-player variant with neutral players.

keyRules.title: Modular Design
keyRules.detail: Each variant/scenario adds or changes specific rules. Pick and combine freely with the base game.

scenarios.name: The Friendly Robber
scenarios.description: The Robber cannot be moved to a hex with a building of a player who only has 2 VP.
details: Protects beginners from early-game targeting

scenarios.name: Harbors of Catan
scenarios.description: Strongest Ports tile (2 VP) for the player with most VP in buildings on ports.
details: Encourages port building strategy

scenarios.name: CATAN Event Cards
scenarios.description: 37-card event deck replaces dice rolls.
details: Cards have production numbers + special events
Events include Earthquake, Epidemic, Good Neighbors
New Year card triggers deck reshuffle

scenarios.name: CATAN for Two
scenarios.description: 2-player variant with neutral players.
details: Double production phase (roll twice)
Trade tokens for forced trades or moving the robber
Must build for neutral players when you build

scenarios.name: Fishing on Catan
scenarios.description: Lake hex replaces desert with fishing grounds on coasts.
details: Fish tokens earned from production
Spend fish: remove robber (2), steal card (3), free resource (4), free road (5), free dev card (7)
Old boot = need +1 VP to win

scenarios.name: Rivers of Catan
scenarios.description: River tiles replace 7 hexes. Bridges cross rivers.
details: Bridge cost: 2 Brick + 1 Wood
Gold coins from river tiles
Wealthiest Catanian (1 VP) / Poorest (-2 VP)

scenarios.name: Merchant Trains
scenarios.description: Wagons form trains from the watering hole.
details: Wagons collected when building, placed via voting
Buildings between 2 wagons = +1 VP each
Wagons count toward Longest Route

scenarios.name: Barbarian Attack
scenarios.description: Castle hex on board. Knights defend against barbarian landings.
details: No robber or base development cards
Knights placed on hex edges
3 barbarians conquer a hex; prisoners = VP

scenarios.name: Trade Routes
scenarios.description: 3 commodity hexes. Wagons transport goods between them.
details: Wagon upgrades increase movement
Gold earned from deliveries
Barbarians block roads

explorers-pirates.name: Catan: Explorers & Pirates
explorers-pirates.description: Set sail to explore unknown waters. Build ships and harbors, complete missions like spice trade or fish hauls, and colonize new lands far from home.

cards.name: Spice Haul
cards.description: Sail your ship to a distant spice island and haul spices back to your home island for Victory Points.
usages: Each completed haul earns 1 VP
Requires reaching and returning from the spice tile

cards.name: Fish Camp
cards.description: Establish a fish camp on a discovered coast to generate fish tokens each round.
usages: Fish tokens can be traded for resources
Position near coastal hexes with good numbers

cards.name: Pirate Lair
cards.description: Discover and destroy pirate lairs scattered across the frontier. Each cleared lair earns 1 Victory Point.
usages: Reach the lair with a ship to clear it
Earns 1 VP immediately upon discovery

setup: Major departure from base game: no development cards, no cities, no robber.
Board has a starting island + 2 unexplored regions (facedown hexes).
First piece is a harbor settlement (on coast, worth 2 VP); second is a regular settlement.
Each player starts with 2 gold coins.
Trade with supply at 3:1 (no ports). Trade 2 gold for 1 resource (twice per turn).

turnOverview.title: 1. Production
turnOverview.detail: Roll dice for resources. Players with no production receive 1 gold instead.

turnOverview.title: 2. Trade & Build
turnOverview.detail: Trade, build settlements/ships, recruit settlers and crews.

turnOverview.title: 3. Ship Movement
turnOverview.detail: Each ship has 4 movement points. Pay 1 Wool for +2 MP. Ships carry settlers, crews, spice sacks, and fish hauls.

keyRules.title: Harbor Settlements
keyRules.detail: Worth 2 VP. Built on coast markers. Can hold 1 large piece or 2 small pieces. Ships must be built adjacent.

keyRules.title: Ships
keyRules.detail: Cost: 1 Wool + 1 Lumber. Have 4 MP per turn. Carry pieces to destinations. Up to 2 ships per edge.

keyRules.title: Exploring
keyRules.detail: Moving a ship adjacent to a facedown hex flips it. Land hexes get a number disc. Gain 1 matching resource (or 2 gold) on discovery.

keyRules.title: Settlers
keyRules.detail: Cost: 1 Grain + 1 Ore. Carried on ships. Build a settlement by unloading at a land hex intersection.

keyRules.title: Crews
keyRules.detail: Cost: 1 Grain + 1 Wool. Used for pirate lair captures and spice farm activation.

keyRules.title: Pirate Ship
keyRules.detail: On a 7, place your pirate ship on a sea hex. Steal 1 resource from a player with a ship there. Moving past costs 1 gold tribute.

keyRules.title: Chase Away Pirate
keyRules.detail: Unmoved ship adjacent to pirate hex rolls 1 die; a 6 chases the pirate away.

scenarios.name: Land Ho!
scenarios.description: Learn exploration basics.
details: Discover hexes, build settlements on new islands

scenarios.name: Pirate Lairs
scenarios.description: Capture pirate lairs on gold field hexes.
details: Place 3 crew to capture a lair
Hero (highest die + crew) advances extra on mission track

scenarios.name: Fish for Catan
scenarios.description: Catch and deliver fish to the Council of Catan.
details: Roll die during movement; matching fish shoal = fish haul
Deliver to Council to advance mission track

scenarios.name: Spices for Catan
scenarios.description: Deliver spice sacks to the Council.
details: Unload crew on spice farm, load spice sack, deliver
Each farm grants a permanent bonus (Swift Voyage, Pirate Bonus, Fast Gold)

scenarios.name: Explorers & Pirates (Full)
scenarios.description: The complete experience with all 3 missions.
details: All missions active simultaneously
Mission track positions and bonus VP tiles contribute to victory
